entry.title: Re: How to extend Supplier Registration Invitation link i...
entry.content:
Hello,

By default is it set to 30 days , if you want to change then edit the field due date field in the questionnaire survey document -

 

For new registrations, the Due Date setting in the Timing Rules section of survey documents for external (supplier-facing) questionnaires defines the amount of time the supplier contact has to fill out the questionnaire after the invitation is sent. If the supplier contact does not fill and submit the questionnaire in that time, the questionnaire closes and the supplier contact can no longer edit it. Reinviting the supplier to register reopens all questionnaires in the registation and resets the due date clock. The default due date value is 30 days, but you can change that time period by editing the Due Date field in the questionnaire survey document. This setting is ignored in internal questionnaires, but it does apply to external registration questionnaires that are sent to internal recipients in an internal registration.

 

More details are available at the below help

https://help.sap.com/docs/strategic-sourcing/supplier-management-setup-and-administration/restrictions-requirements-and-helpful-hints-for-setting-up-supplier-registration-project-template-f3babc19dff34f56b6102172822fa386

I have created a RAP based Fiori app and deployed it to my On Prem system.

So, how do we set the fiori launchpad configuration to directly jump to create object page for the app with draft enabled.

Could you please help me to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @albertosimeoni, Some HANA ML logic can indeed be embedded into Datasphere table functions, see https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/embedding-hana-machine-learning-models-into-sap-data-warehouse-cloud-views/ba-p/13566332 Please note the comments in that blog's summary though on limitations / alternatives.

This customer influence idea should also be relevant for your question. Please add your name to the list if you think this would be useful (and I agree, it would indeed be very valuable to have this implemented).

Cloud Foundry is just an example that I know works well for a number of customers. It's lightweight and comes at low cost for this scheduling scenario. The Discovery Center gives a pricing estimate for

  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • SAP Job Scheduling Service

I know of some customer using their operation system's scheduler. But you can also use AI Core or really any environment that can schedule Python code.

entry.title: Many External Credit Information Providers, One Solution: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Credit Integration
entry.content:
This blog post aims to give you a short introduction to SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration and points you to the official SAP documentation for the product.

What's the business need?

When you assess the credit risk of your business partners, external credit information is key to your business. There are several credit agencies out there, such as Schufa, Dun & Bradstreet, or Creditreform, who provide you with up-to-date data and thereby help you minimize potential risks for your company.

Pain points: You always want the latest data from these credit agencies, and you don't want to manually maintain and assess this data in your systems. Therefore, you want an automated process to import the credit information into your system. However, connecting these credit agencies one-by-one to your SAP Credit Management system can be complicated because every credit agency has their individual integration set-up, and the maintenance on top can be time-consuming. 

What's the credit integration solution about?

The idea of SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration is simple: You connect your SAP Credit Management system once to the credit integration solution, which in turn is connected to several credit agencies that can provide you with their external credit information. This credit information is then forwarded back into your SAP Credit Management system.

Your benefit: With integrating only the credit integration solution, you have access to all credit agencies which are connected to it, without the before-mentioned overhead. At the time of writing this blog post, it connects you to 15 different credit agencies. You can see the current list of connected credit agencies here: Integrated Credit Agencies and Products 

 

What does this look like for end users?

Because the credit integration solution is integrated with SAP Credit Management, credit controllers can access the solution from the credit profile of their business partner in the Manage Credit Accounts app:

       More information: Accessing Credit Reports from Your SAP Credit Management System 

They get forwarded to the Manage External Credit Information app, which is part of the credit integration solution. Here, they can see all external credit information for their business partner and order new products:

They can also find the business partner in the database of the credit agency, to assign the credit-agency specific ID to the business partner. This enables them to place the order at the credit agency.

In the Find Business Partner app, credit controllers can also look for any other business partner in the database of the credit agencies. To do this, they can select the country of their business partner...

...and the credit agency they want to get the information from:

 

Do you want more information?
  • For detailed step-by-step descriptions about how to work with the credit integration solution, see our User Guide.
  • Shortlink to our product page: https://help.sap.com/creditintegration
  • Find other blog posts using the SAP S4HANA Cloud for credit integration tag.
     
Do you have any questions or comments?

If you have any questions, feedback, or comments, don't hesitate to post them in the comments section below. We'd love to hear from you!

entry.title: Troubleshooting: Ordering a Credit Product from an External Credit Agency
entry.content:
You're a credit controller and are using the SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration solution to receive external credit information to assess the credit risk of your business partners.
You want to order a credit product from an external credit agency using one of the apps: Find Business Partner or Manage External Credit Information. However, you stumble over some problems while doing so.

Knowing where to address your issues when using the app can help to save you some time.
Depending on your problem, you have to contact one of these parties:

  • Your administrator:
    When you have to adjust the setup of your solution.
  • The credit agency:
    When there’s an issue with their connection or database.
  • SAP:
    When there’s an error with the credit integration solution itself.

Troubleshooting

 

I receive this error message: "Response from credit agency: You are not entitled to any of the available Products (or) Tier Levels. Please contact your D&B account representative."

➡️ Contact the credit agency.
Usually, this error occurs when your account at the credit agency itself hasn't been set up properly. When communicating with the credit agency, the credit integration solution is denied the right to order a product. It means that this permission is missing for your contract with the credit agency, which is represented by your credit agency account.
Therefore, ask the credit agency to verify the settings for your credit agency account on their side.

I want to order a specific product, but it's missing on the selection screen.

➡️ Contact your Administrator.
Your administrator should first check if the desired product is activated in the Configure Credit Agencies and Products app:

More information: Activating Credit Products 
If the desired product is activated, then:

➡️ Contact the credit agency.
It is possible that not all products are made available by them, for example, due to country restrictions. Ask them for more information.

I receive an error message that the update to the back-end system failed.

➡️ Check out this blog post: Sorry, we're not done yet - the link will follow 🙂 

I receive an error message with a response from the credit agency.

➡️ Contact the credit agency for any error message which contains the phrase “Response from the credit agency”.
The credit integration solution by SAP is working properly but ran into a problem when it tried to communicate with the credit agency. The error message provides you with more details on what went wrong there. Please pass on this error message to the credit agency and ask them to check their logs to solve this issue.

There's data missing in the credit information or PDF which I received from the credit agency.

For example, the legal form is missing, which was provided in a previous report by the credit agency.

➡️ Check for yourself: 
If you're missing some information on a credit report PDF, you can double-check for the data in the XML report sent by the credit agency. To do this, choose Show XML (or Show JSON) when you display the PDF:

➡️ Contact the credit agency.
Check with the respective credit agency if they delivered the complete information. Usually, since sending the information is part of their responsibility, they can solve this error.
If they confirm that they did send the complete information and there must be a technical issue, you can:

➡️ Contact SAP. 
Open a support case for the following component: FIN-FSCM-CAI
Important: Provide all the details in the support case! This helps us to identify the problem.

I get an error message with a correlation ID.

For example: "Unable to search for companies in the database of the credit agency. Please try again later. Correlation ID: (…)"

➡️ Contact SAP.
There is something wrong with the app. It can be a temporary problem, on which SAP is already working.
However, if the problem persists, contact SAP. Open a support case for the following component: FIN-FSCM-CAI
Important: Copy the Correlation ID text (not a screenshot) from the error message and include it in the support case! This helps us to identify the problem.

 

Do you want more information?
  • Shortlink to our product page: 
    https://help.sap.com/creditintegration
  • Related end-user documentation: 
    Ordering Credit Reports 
  • Find other troubleshooting blog posts using the SAP S4HANA Cloud for credit integration tag.
     
Do you have any questions or comments?

If you have any questions, feedback, or comments, don't hesitate to post them in the comments section below. We'd love to hear from you!


– This article is co-authored by Dominik G. and @Christina_WS. –

entry.title: Troubleshooting: Finding a Business Partner in the Database of a Credit Agency
entry.content:
You're a credit controller and are using the SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration solution to receive external credit information to assess the credit risk of your business partners.
You want to find a business partner in the database of a company by searching for it in the Find Business Partner app. However, you stumble over some problems in the app.

Knowing where to address your issues when using the app can help to save you some time.
Depending on your problem, you have to contact one of these parties:

  • Your administrator:
    When you have to adjust the setup of your solution.
  • The credit agency:
    When there’s an issue with their connection or database.
  • SAP:
    When there’s an error with the credit integration solution itself.

Troubleshooting:


I can’t use the app. When I open it, I get this error message:

"You're not authorized for any credit agency. Please ask your administrator to assign your user to a user group."

➡️ Contact your administrator.
Your user is not included in a user group, or your user group is not authorized for any credit agency.
More information: Maintaining User Groups

I can’t select a specific credit agency for my selected country.

The credit agency is not available for selection in the dropdown-menu.

➡️ Contact your administrator.
Your user isn’t included in a user group with access to that credit agency in that country.
More information: Maintaining User Groups

The search fields in the app change depending on the agency and country.

➡️ No need to worry! This is the normal behavior of the app.
The fields in the upper section of the app are always visible and provided by SAP.
The fields in the lower section of the app are provided by the credit agency you have selected. Each credit agency decides on which search fields they want to offer. Therefore, the user interface changes depending on the credit agency you have selected.

When I search for a company, I don’t find the expected results. I think an entry is missing.

➡️ Contact the credit agency.
SAP doesn’t have any influence on the list of results in the app. These are provided by the credit agency. If you think that an entry for a company is missing, contact the credit agency.
Maybe you have to order an investigation at the credit agency.
More information: Requesting an Investigation for a Business Partner  

There’s an error message when I search for a company.

For example: “We couldn't establish the connection to the credit agency. Response from the credit agency: You are not entitled to any Product or Tier Level.“

➡️ Contact the credit agency for any error message which contains the phrase “Response from the credit agency”.
The credit integration solution by SAP is working properly but ran into a problem when it tried to communicate with the credit agency. The error message provides you with more details on what went wrong there. Please pass on this error message to the credit agency and ask them to check their logs to solve this issue.

I get an error message. What can I do?

For example: "Unable to search for companies in the database of the credit agency. Please try again later. Correlation ID: (…)"

➡️ Contact SAP.
There is something wrong with the app. It can be a temporary problem, on which SAP is already working.
However, if the problem persists, contact SAP. Open a support case for the following component: FIN-FSCM-CAI
Important: Copy the Correlation ID text (not a screenshot) from the error message and include it in the support case! This helps us to identify the problem.

 

Do you want more information?
  • Shortlink to our product page: 
    https://help.sap.com/creditintegration
  • Related end-user documentation: 
    Finding a Company ID for a Business Partner 
  • Find other troubleshooting blog posts using the SAP S4HANA Cloud for credit integration tag.
Do you have any questions or comments?

If you have any questions, feedback, or comments, don't hesitate to post them in the comments section below. We'd love to hear from you!


– This article is co-authored by Dominik G. and @Christina_WS. –

entry.title: Monthly planned depreciation correction
entry.content:
I create an asset in SAP today.

Straightline, 10 years, depreciation start 1.1.2016, it means end of life is 31.12.2025. I capitalize the asset with value 43.226.892 (posting date Sep because can't post to the past). Planned posted depreciation:

What can I do (unplanned depreciation, manual depreciation, revaluation etc.) to get the monthly (Oct-Nov-Dec) planned amount to 360.224,10 please? FY24 NBV must be 4.322.689,20. I've tried unplanned depreciation so that FY24 NBV is 4.322.689,20 but monthly is not as expected:

Thank you, Gurus.

entry.title: Intermittent Issue with Java Application in SAP SuccessFactors Extension
entry.content:
We have developed a Java-servlet based extension application for SAP SuccessFactors. Our deployment package includes both the Java application and the Approuter application, which customers deploy through the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment. We use Approuter for authentication, and once a request is successfully authenticated, it is forwarded to our Java application.

Although the application generally operates smoothly, one of our customers occasionally experiences the issue described below (which may occur once a month or even more frequently). This problem is resolved when the application is manually restarted.

Could you please assist us in identifying the root cause of this issue?

  1. When an application URL (via approuter) is accessed, the application logs the following error in BTP:

entry.title: Re: Request additional Special G/l indicator in SAP s/4ha...
entry.content:
The creation of a new special G/L indicator is considered to be a non-standard expert configuration. This is a customer-specific request, so it needs to be followed by the review process for non-standard configuration request. For which you need to create a WCR request contacting your Product Expert, e-CSP or CSP. If the WCR is approved, please create a new case under component XX-S4C-SRV-CON (Expert Configuration Team).

Kindly refer KBA 2761212 - Create special G/L indicator in public cloud system

entry.title: Calculate Tax amount different from the SAP standard
entry.content: A down payment was posted for a forward order and partial quantities were calculated. An item (item 30) of the forward order was then canceled. Since the down payment is posted with the full value to the last order item, the reversal of the order item and the reversal of the posted down payment result in a data inconsistency in the area of down payments/taxes. From our point of view, the inconsistency results from the tax calculation based on the net value of the delivery - which in our view is not correct in this case - and not from the net value of the delivery reduced by the new down payment. We are looking for a way to avoid this inconsistency by default (i.e. to calculate the tax in such cases differently from the standard).

entry.title: Re: System does not allow posting from EWM to IM-MM if th...
entry.content:
Hello,

Find below options available and I am not sure where you are maintaining batch in ERP

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you are processing the inbound or outbound delivery, EWM copies the batch characteristics to the delivery, for example, the country or region of origin, shelf life expiration date, or the vendor batch number.

When EWM receives a delivery, the delivery may contain a batch number from outside of EWM. Alternatively, you can specify the batch number directly in EWM. EWM determines where the batch information originates from and whether you can still change the batch number.

To find this information, on the launchpad, choose Extended Warehouse Management  Delivery Processing  Inbound Delivery  Maintain Inbound Deliveries or Extended Warehouse Management  Delivery Processing  Outbound Delivery  Outbound Delivery Orders. In the form view, you can see for your delivery item the Value Determination Indicator next to the Batch field. This indicator tells you where the batch number originates from and whether you can change it.

The following options exist:

  • A different system specifies a batch number, for example in a sales order in SAP ERP. You must use this batch number - that is, you cannot change the specified batch number.

  • A different system proposes a batch number. For example, you have entered the batch number in the inbound delivery of SAP ERP. You can change this batch number.

  • You specify a batch number manually in EWM in the apps Maintain Inbound Deliveries or Outbound Delivery Orders.

    • You can choose an existing batch number and enter it in the Batch field.

    • You can have EWM create new batch numbers for inbound deliveries. For this, leave the Batch field empty and choose the function Create Batch. As soon as you save the inbound delivery, EWM creates a new batch number.

For outbound deliveries, you can also specify the batch number in the corresponding warehouse task. EWM copies the batch number to the delivery. Depending on your settings, EWM writes the confirmed quantity and batch number directly into the delivery item, or it creates a batch subitem with the confirmed quantity and batch number.

entry.title: GR/IR Inventories - Balance Summary report shows values in amount awaiting invoice/receipt

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GR/IR Inventories - Balance Summary report shows valu...
entry.content:
Since the purchase order status still showing as partially delivered / invoiced and mark for final clearing was also not set, system is still assuming that goods/invoices are expected, so the same is being shown in the report and also the item is not marked for final clearing.

Should an item to be finished before it has been fully delivered or invoiced, the item can be manually marked for final clearing.

You can manually mark for final clearing when:

  • An item is not completed delivered or invoiced.
  • No further transactions are expected for the item.

Follow the below steps:

  1. Go to the Inventory Valuation work center. 
  2. Go to the Master Data view.
  3. Go to the Purchasing Document Items subview.
  4. Show All Purchase Order Items.
  5. Find the respective purchase order item and select item. 
  6. Choose the Final Clearing button and select Mark. 
  7. Enter the final clearing date. 

The item will be included in the next GR/IR Clearing Run, provided its final clearing date is in the accounting period selected for the run.

entry.title: Re: How to navigate directly to the commercial tab of the...
entry.content:
Solution found!

In my case, I was incorrectly filling in the parameters of the cl_bupa_navigation_request class.

To call the BP transaction via the start_with_navigation method and display the business view directly, we must add the following line to the code above:

lo_request->set_bupa_sub_header_id( 'CVIC01' ).   " Customer: General data


In case you want to start the transaction with another view, you can check in table TBZ3A which is the correct parameter for your view.

Also, following the same procedure you can select the sub-tab of the view, using the following method:

lo_request->set_bupa_sub_header_tab( ' ' ).

entry.title: Getting stream data using cds.RemoteService in CAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a question regarding reading stream data with CAP. I have this remote service endpoint, which should return a stream (Content-Type header is "application/octet-stream").

In CAP I am sending the request as follows:

const fs = require('fs');

let myRemoteService = await cds.connect.to('MY_REMOTE_SERVICE');

let response = await myRemoteService.send({
    method: 'GET',
    path: path,
    headers: headers
});

// specifying the encoding doesn't change anything (= file is never correct)
let b = Buffer.from(response);
fs.writeFileSync('myfile.ext', b);

The data I am getting back is always a UTF-8 encoded String and it does not have the correct data anymore (probably due to encoding). If I try to write the response to a file, I only get a corrupted file (as a string or buffer).

However, if I send the request in Postman and saved the response to a file, the content is still correct.

I also found out that I can get the correct content using Axios, if I specify the response to be a string.

const axios = require('axios');
const fs = require('fs');

...

this.on('func1', async req => {
  let r = axios({
    method: 'get',
    url: requestUrl,
    headers: headers,
    responseType: 'stream' // THIS IS THE KEY!
  })

  let res = await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    let s = r.data;
    let b = [];

    s.on('data', data => {
      b.push(data);
    });

    s.on('end', () => {
      b = Buffer.concat(b);
      fs.writeFileSync('myfile.ext', b); // works
      resolve(b);
    });
  });

  return btoa(res);
});

I know that SAP Cloud SDK HTTP Client, which CAP internally uses, is based on Axios.

So my question is: Is there a way to specify the Axios request config, when sending requests in CAP.

Or is there any other way to get the data as a stream? 

Thank you very much.

Best regards,
Atakan

entry.title: Got security? Tune in to our sessions at SAP TechEd 2024!
entry.content:
If you want to find out more about the security services available with SAP BTP, you've come to the right place. Let me point you to the three sessions you should attend for a concise summary of the most important SAP BTP security features and strategies. 

Start off with XP103 | Got security? Services for security and compliance with SAP BTP and hear from Michael Friedrich, Chief Product Owner for SAP BTP security, and Nathalie Bergstroem, SAP BTP compliance developer, which security services you should check out. You will learn about the key principles for developing robust applications that comply with industry regulations, using secure development practices and compliance services. Nathalie will take you on a deep dive into the SAP Data Privacy Integration service, as well as our SAP Audit Log service architecture.

Next up are two sessions that focus on identity and access management. For the first one, we've teamed up with our partner Microsoft. Martin Raepple, technical program manager at Microsoft, and I will explain how your hybrid environments can benefit from our collaboration in XP202 | Identity lifecycle management with SAP BTP and Microsoft Entra. We will show you how to combine SAP Cloud Identity Services with Microsoft Entra, a universal identity platform, to create a holistic user lifecycle management for both SAP and non-SAP solutions, in the cloud and on premise.

The last session is a special treat, brought to you right from the people who know our customer implementations best. Two of our expert security consultants, Oxana Junkereit and Tobias Lejczyk, will share some of their extensive expertise in XP203 | SAP Cloud Identity Services: Best practices for a central user management. Learn how to get started, how to benefit from SAP Cloud Identity Services, and explore the concepts and capabilities of these core SAP BTP services. 

Check out all the other sessions SAP TechEd has to offer in our session catalog, and make sure you read the Real Talk with Stephanie and Konstantin | SAP Business Technology Platform Track Leads for some background and recommendations on our other track sessions!

entry.title: Re: SFTP adapter - how to handle errors within processing a file
entry.content:
Hi David, that is a great question, as my thinking went the same way and I wanted to handle everything in a single iflow. Nevertheless, it was impossible to implement the retry mechanism in such way. Either the iflow ends up in error and terminates, or we handle the error in exception subprocess and retry it from there. But what if it fails again? We would be catching error in exception subprocess in another exception subprocess and that would be impossible or clumsy.

I like the method of decoupling, as you can have for example a single iflow for saving files generally and just fill it with header parameters from various iflows which need to save a file. Another example is sending emails, and you configure the email at one place.

The wait is there just to give the SFTP server some time to recover in case it is down for some reason. I can imagine a scenario, where instead of setting a wait time, you set another URL of a backup SFTP server, which will be used for a second attempt.

entry.title: Re: RAP - Error on validation message highlight
entry.content:
Hi rodrigo,

I had the same problem and I found the solution for this problem during debugging in Frontend.

You need to add %is_draft = '01' in your reported-child structure, because the UI is filtering on IsActiveEntity=true in the Object Page Table as you are still in draft mode when filling failed.

Cheers
Andreas

entry.title: Treasury incoming payments Reconciliation Automation
entry.content:
According to the standard SAP TRM process,  the incoming payments in Treasury deals put together and accounting posting is processed in two legs, one to the Balance sheet and other to the Bank Clearing/interim GL account. At a GL account level, it would be a tedious task to reconcile incoming payment from counterparty. There would be debit/credit posted through TRM sub module using TBB1 which, has to be reconciled with debit/credit posted through Bank statement incoming payment posting related to TRM deals.

User Story

Anna is a financial analyst responsible for processing Treasury incoming payments. Her day starts with a huge number of bank statements that are processed and treasury incoming GL, posting getting credited into Bank clearing GL account.

Anna spends half of her time, during the day getting the details of incoming payment settlements received on a bank statement and reconciling the amounts posted from sub-module TRM. She reports the challenge to her supervisor Rohan.

Rohan understood her challenge and the efforts spent by Anna on a daily basis. Rohan reaches to IT Consultant to provide a Solution. Lee is an IT Consultant who understood Anna’s challenge and proposes an RPA (Robotic Process Automation) to Rohan.

Business Requirement

When a treasury deal for Forex spot (USD buy and EUR sell) is entered into the system, the transaction has two legs.

  1. Currency EUR outgoing payment is processed and accounted.
  2. Currency USD incoming payment is processed and accounted.

According to the above scenario,  with USD incoming, likewise there could be many more treasury deals such investments, borrowing, commodity swaps that happen in USD currency for which,  there could be multiple transaction happening with multiple Counterparties. For example,

  1.  Currency 10000 USD incoming payment against Forex spot with counterparty BKDTBANDKE (Deutsche)
  2. Currency 10000 USD incoming payment against Forex spot with counterparty CITIBANK
  3. Currency 20000 USD incoming payment against Forex spot with counterparty CITIBANK

As we have received on a bank statement two Credit

  1. USD 10000 with payment note BKDTBANDKE (Deutsche)
  2. USD 10000 with payment note CITIBANK

The incoming payments USD10000 and USD 10000 have be cleared against the open item posted through TRM posting.

At any point of time Anna should be able retrieve the incoming payments from Counterparty,  based on due date which are not settled by counterparty.

Fig 1 – TRM Clearing GL accounts posted through TBB1

Automation required to clear open items in TRM clearing and when incoming payments are processed on a bank statement posting.

Fig 2 – TRM Clearing GL accounts posted and cleared through TBB1 and Bank statement

SAP Solution

Lee , the IT architecture had proposed a solution, when treasury deal is entered, treasury deal should have a unique Account Assignment Reference (AAR) which identifies the Counterparty. So that when treasury deal is posted to accounting (TBB1), a separate Clearing GL account based on counterparty is posted. Incoming treasury payments are identified with counterparty on bank statement.

When a bank statement is processed, the posting rule should be defined to clear the open item/post in unique Clearing GL account with counterparty and debit the main bank account.

Account Assignment reference solution is not restricted to a Product type, all deals with an Account assignment reference with a Counterparty, will have the above solution works.

How can we  achieve the above process automation

  1. In customization for Account Assignment Reference setting, Account assignment reference will be created specific to Counterparty
  2. In customization for OT83, we define posting rules to post and clear the GL account for clearing Counterparty.

User end transactions link

https://1drv.ms/v/s!Ah2KZic1YUA_gZh529MlUoNKPwxEMw?e=8EsFTb 

Customization:-

Fig 3 – Account Assignment Reference Configuration setup

Fig 4 – GL Accounts assignment to Account symbol (specific to Counterparty)

Fig 5 – Searching string setup for posting to Counterparty bank clearing account

Fig 6 – Assignment of Search string to Co code configuration setup

Fig 7 – Configuration setup of OT83 Accounts symbols

Fig 8 – Define posting rules

MT940 sample

Fig 9 – Bank statement sample format

Fig 10 – GL Account posting

Customization link

https://1drv.ms/v/s!Ah2KZic1YUA_gZh4NMjf-I_8ZKCuFw?e=8AVQjy 

Disclaimer

Please check with bank to populate reference of Counterparty reference, so that search string can be configured for automation. In case, if we do not get data on bank statement, posting can be without clear open item. Later, open items should be manually cleared.

Conclusion

Treasury incoming payments can be reconciled at a counterparty level. As the sub-module Treasury posting into GL is  identified at a Counterparty level. Similarly, when bank statements are processed and posted into GL, posting will be into GL identified at a Counterparty level.

entry.title: Programmatically Change/Update the Local SAP GUI Options
entry.content:
To all the Guru's,

When trying to set-up new users in SAP, there are things that can be done with Scripting and things that I have yet to figure out how to do.  I'm not ABAP programmer, I can barely create a script to update / data using Excel.  But using Power Points and scripting does nothing to ease the user into setting up their profile within SAP,  As a matter of course, I see that most users fail miserably in getting the User Profile set-up properly and so they struggle with using SAP.

\Having said all that, my question to all is this;

Is there a a way to programmatically change the SAP GUI Options?

I would like to send a user an email with a link or a file that would make all of the changes in their profile for them and to also change things in the SAP GUI Options (Customize Local Layout).

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Slipstream hide "choose" mobile Screen
entry.content:
Hi,

There are two options: 

  1. Change input field in Flavor Editor and set properties - "showTypeAhead=false" and "historyDisabled=true"
  2. Or use "sap-se-valuesuggest" url parameter to do this for all fields. Url parameter is described in https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SCREEN_PERSONAS/ea09d73800744056a761296a70e357f2/81527801fafd4695b28ab19309ec13a1.html

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: QM Open Note – #4 – RFC Function Modules for Downloading / Uploading and Record types
entry.content:
Dear All,

we just tested QIRF_GET_ORIGINAL_VALUES2 with Q84 to create a new insp.point with external ID

But 🙂

we than used QIRF_GET_ORIGINAL_VALUES2 with Q55 to valuate and close insp. characteristics. Will not work (?). Characteristic was only set to status "2" - not to "5"

with QIRF_GET_FEATURE_VALUES2 and Q84 we "moved" this inspec. characteristic to status "3" ... but for later automatic usage decision we need "5"

Any ideas?

even, why to use three different function modules in four steps, to get an inspection done? It's not really smart, isn't it?

Like to hear any ideas 🙂

Bye,

Marcus

entry.title: Re: XML Simple Transformation read attributes
entry.content:
I did find solution by myself finally. 

This syntax will do the job: 

           <Fuel>
              <tt:loop ref="FUEL">
                <FuelConsumption>
                  <tt:attribute name="unit" value-ref="$REF.unit"/>
                  <tt:value ref="FuelConsumption"/>
                </FuelConsumption>
              </tt:loop>
             </Fuel>

entry.title: Update language of sales order item description and billing document item description
entry.content:
Dear community,

System: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 2408

I have created a sales document, e.g. a sales order for a customer. In the business partner the country and standard communication language are set to DE for German language.

The text for the item description (from material master) in the sales order will now appear in DE language on all sales documents including billing documents. Even if I change language in the output items to EN the item description will always be displayed in the language of the customer, the rest of the form will change to EN.

Now I change the country and language of the customer e.g. to US and EN. All new sales documents and billing documents will be printed with EN item description taken from material master.

BUT: The old sales documents will still be printed with DE item description, the same is valid for new billing documents created from old sales documents after the change language in customer master. 

How can I change this behavior? There is an option for price updates, how can I mass update the item description in existing sales documents and downstream documents?

The system must always use the latest language entry from the business partner to determine the item language for description from material master.

Thank you.

BR

Dirk

entry.title: Share your insights from the SAP Transformation Excellence Summits 2024
entry.content:
Dear community members, 

With the upcoming SAP Transformation Excellence Summits that are taking place in Gold Coast (ongoing this week), Chicago (Sept 16-18), and Frankfurt (Oct 7-9), it’s a perfect opportunity for us to gather your overall experience and thoughts on the things you learned at the summit.  

Whether you attended one of the events in person or participated virtually, we’d love to hear your impressions. Here are a few guiding questions to spark the discussions: 

  • What were some key takeaways or standout sessions for you? 
  • How do you think the insights shared can be applied to drive business transformation within your organization? 
  • Did you network with any interesting peers or experts who provided new perspectives? 
  • Were there any particular technologies or strategies presented that caught your attention? 
  • How do you envision the future of business transformation after attending the summit? 

Your shared experiences and insights can be incredibly valuable for our community. Looking forward to reading your comments!
